---
title: "Emissions & Energy"
description: "CO2 emissions, energy, and environmental indicators — Canada and OECD peers"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import os
from pipeline.config import (
    CANADA_COLOR, PEER_COLOR, HIGHLIGHT_WIDTH, PEER_WIDTH,
    HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY, PEER_COUNTRIES, get_data_date, GLOBAL_CONTEXT_COLORS, FUEL_COLORS,
)
from pipeline.charts import peer_comparison_line, ranked_bar
```

How much carbon Canada emits, how that has changed over time, and how its energy is sourced — shown against OECD peers. Production-based measures count emissions where they physically occur.

## Where Canada stands

A scorecard of where Canada ranks among the 17 peers on each measure (latest available year). Dots are ordered by rank, so **further right is more favourable for Canada** (lower-is-better measures are flipped accordingly). Canada is red, key comparators in colour; hover a dot for its value and rank.

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pipeline.charts import page_snapshot
page_snapshot("environment").show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
```

## Greenhouse gas emissions

Canada's total greenhouse gas emissions — all gases, on the National Inventory Report basis (the official series). Emissions have drifted down from their mid-2000s peak. Successive federal governments have committed to reducing emissions, though progress has remained challenging.

```{python}
#| echo: false
ghg_path = "data/environment/eccc_ghg.csv"
if os.path.exists(ghg_path):
    g = pd.read_csv(ghg_path)
    figg = go.Figure()
    figg.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=g["year"], y=g["ghg_mt"], mode="lines", name="Emissions",
        line=dict(color=CANADA_COLOR, width=3), hovertemplate="%{x}: %{y:.0f} Mt<extra></extra>"))
    figg.update_layout(plot_bgcolor="white", height=460, hovermode="x unified",
        yaxis=dict(title="GHG emissions (Mt CO2e)", gridcolor="#e0e0e0", rangemode="tozero", ticksuffix=" Mt"),
        xaxis=dict(title="", gridcolor="#e0e0e0", dtick=5),
        margin=dict(t=30, b=115, r=20))
    figg.add_annotation(
        text=f"Source: Environment and Climate Change Canada, National Inventory Report (CESI) | Data as of: {get_data_date(ghg_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.18, showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))
    figg.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("GHG data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Greenhouse gas emissions by economic sector

Where the emissions come from. Oil and gas and transport are the two largest sources; electricity has fallen as provinces phased out coal.

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pipeline.charts import stacked_area
ghg_sec_path = "data/environment/eccc_ghg_by_sector.csv"
if os.path.exists(ghg_sec_path):
    gs = pd.read_csv(ghg_sec_path)
    fig = stacked_area(gs, "year", "ghg_mt", "sector",
        yaxis_title="GHG emissions (Mt CO2e)", yticksuffix=" Mt", hover_decimals=0,
        source_note=f"Source: Environment and Climate Change Canada (CESI) | Data as of: {get_data_date(ghg_sec_path)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("GHG-by-sector data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## CO2 emissions per capita

Production-based CO2 emissions per person, in tonnes of CO2. This is the most directly comparable measure of a country's carbon footprint, controlling for population size.

```{python}
#| echo: false
co2cap_path = "data/environment/oecd_co2_per_capita.csv"
if os.path.exists(co2cap_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2cap_path)

    fig = peer_comparison_line(
        df, x_col="year", y_col="co2_per_capita",
        title="", yaxis_title="Tonnes CO2 per capita",
    )
    fig.update_layout(
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2cap_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("CO2 per capita data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Latest CO2 per capita: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(co2cap_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2cap_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "co2_per_capita", "Tonnes CO2 per capita",
        f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2cap_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f} t CO2/capita<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## CO2 per capita in global context

The comparison above is among wealthy countries, where Canada sits at the high end. Widening the lens to the whole world changes the picture. Canada and the United States are among the highest per-capita emitters anywhere; China's emissions per person have climbed steeply since 2000 to around the world's upper-middle, while India's remain low. The world average (dashed) sits well below all of them — high per-capita emissions are a feature of rich, energy-intensive economies. (China and India are shown here for global context; they are not part of the OECD peer group used elsewhere.)

```{python}
#| echo: false
co2glob_path = "data/environment/owid_co2_global_context.csv"
if os.path.exists(co2glob_path):
    g = pd.read_csv(co2glob_path)
    STYLE = {"CAN": (CANADA_COLOR, 3, "solid"), "USA": (GLOBAL_CONTEXT_COLORS["USA"], 2, "solid"),
             "CHN": (GLOBAL_CONTEXT_COLORS["CHN"], 2, "solid"), "IND": (GLOBAL_CONTEXT_COLORS["IND"], 2, "solid"),
             "WLD": (GLOBAL_CONTEXT_COLORS["WLD"], 2, "dot")}
    ORDER = [("CAN", "Canada"), ("USA", "United States"), ("CHN", "China"),
             ("IND", "India"), ("WLD", "World average")]
    fig = go.Figure()
    for code, label in ORDER:
        s = g[g["country_code"] == code].sort_values("year")
        col, w, dash = STYLE[code]
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=s["year"], y=s["co2_per_capita"], name=label,
            mode="lines", line=dict(color=col, width=w, dash=dash),
            hovertemplate=f"{label}: %{{y:.1f}} t<extra></extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(plot_bgcolor="white", height=460, hovermode="x unified",
        xaxis=dict(title="", gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        yaxis=dict(title="Tonnes CO2 per capita", gridcolor="#e0e0e0", rangemode="tozero"),
        legend=dict(orientation="v", yanchor="top", y=1, xanchor="left", x=1.02),
        margin=dict(t=30, b=90, r=160),
        annotations=[dict(text=f"Source: Our World in Data (Global Carbon Project) | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2glob_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.22, showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Global-context CO2 data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## CO2 emissions trend (indexed, 2000 = 100)

How each country's CO2 emissions have changed relative to 2000 levels. Values below 100 indicate reductions since 2000; above 100 indicate growth.

```{python}
#| echo: false
co2idx_path = "data/environment/oecd_co2_indexed.csv"
if os.path.exists(co2idx_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2idx_path)

    fig = peer_comparison_line(
        df, x_col="year", y_col="co2_index",
        title="", yaxis_title="Index (2000 = 100)",
    )
    # Add reference line at 100
    fig.add_hline(y=100, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888", opacity=0.5)
    fig.update_layout(
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2idx_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("CO2 indexed data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Consumption-based CO2 per capita

The per-capita measure above counts emissions where they physically occur (production-based). This consumption-based measure instead assigns emissions to where goods are finally consumed, so the carbon embodied in imported goods counts against the importer rather than the exporter. Comparing the two shows whether trade flatters or worsens a country's footprint — relevant for a trading economy like Canada's.

```{python}
#| echo: false
cco2_path = "data/environment/owid_consumption_co2.csv"
if os.path.exists(cco2_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(cco2_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "consumption_co2_pc",
        title="", yaxis_title="Tonnes CO2 per capita (consumption-based)", show_average=True)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: Our World in Data (Global Carbon Project) | Data as of: {get_data_date(cco2_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34, showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Consumption-based CO2 data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest consumption-based CO2: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(cco2_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(cco2_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "consumption_co2_pc", "Tonnes CO2 per capita",
        f"Source: Our World in Data (Global Carbon Project) | Data as of: {get_data_date(cco2_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f} t<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## CO2 emissions intensity

Emissions per dollar of economic output — how much CO2 the economy releases to produce a given amount of GDP. It is one gauge of how carbon-heavy growth is, and most peers have brought it down steadily while their economies grew. Canada runs among the highest in the group: a cold climate, long transport distances, and an energy- and resource-heavy economy all raise the carbon released per dollar of output.

```{python}
#| echo: false
co2gdp_path = "data/environment/owid_co2_per_gdp.csv"
if os.path.exists(co2gdp_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2gdp_path)
    df = df[df["year"] >= 1990]
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "co2_per_gdp",
        title="", yaxis_title="kg CO2 per $ of GDP", show_average=True)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: Our World in Data (Global Carbon Project) | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2gdp_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34, showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("CO2 intensity data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest CO2 emissions intensity: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(co2gdp_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2gdp_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "co2_per_gdp", "kg CO2 per $ of GDP",
        f"Source: Our World in Data (Global Carbon Project) | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2gdp_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.2f} kg per $<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Electricity generation per capita

How much electricity a society generates per person is one of the most reliable physical markers of material living standards — the "energy ladder" that tracks closely with income, industrialization, and the comforts (heating, cooling, appliances, transport, heavy industry) that electricity makes possible. By this measure Canada sits near the very top of the world: about **16,000 kWh per person each year** — second among peers only to hydro-rich Norway, roughly **four times the world average** and more than ten times India's. Some of that reflects prosperity, but much of it reflects a cold climate, vast distances, abundant low-cost hydro power, and an energy-intensive resource and manufacturing base — so it is best read as a *driver and correlate* of living standards rather than a pure measure of them.

```{python}
#| echo: false
epc_path = "data/environment/owid_electricity_per_capita.csv"
if os.path.exists(epc_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(epc_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "per_capita_electricity",
        title="", yaxis_title="Electricity generation per capita (kWh)",
        show_average=True, initial_start=2000,
        source_note=f"Source: Our World in Data (Energy Institute, Ember, EIA) | Data as of: {get_data_date(epc_path)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Electricity-per-capita data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest electricity generation per capita: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(epc_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(epc_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "per_capita_electricity", "Electricity generation per capita (kWh)",
        f"Source: Our World in Data (Energy Institute, Ember, EIA) | Data as of: {get_data_date(epc_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:,.0f} kWh<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Low-carbon electricity

The share of electricity generated from non-fossil sources — hydro, nuclear, wind, and solar combined. This is the measure most relevant to decarbonising the grid: it counts nuclear (which "renewables" alone leaves out), and unlike total energy it isn't dominated by the oil and gas still burned for transport and heating.

```{python}
#| echo: false
emix_path = "data/environment/owid_energy_mix.csv"
if os.path.exists(emix_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(emix_path)
    df = df[df["low_carbon_share_elec"].notna()]
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "low_carbon_share_elec",
        title="", yaxis_title="% of electricity from low-carbon sources",
        show_average=True)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: Our World in Data (Energy Institute, Ember) | Data as of: {get_data_date(emix_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Electricity data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest low-carbon electricity: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(emix_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(emix_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "low_carbon_share_elec", "% of electricity from low-carbon sources",
        f"Source: Our World in Data | Data as of: {get_data_date(emix_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.0f}%<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Electricity mix by country

How each country generates electricity, by source (latest available year). Fossil fuels (coal, oil, gas) versus nuclear and renewables (which include hydro). Countries are sorted cleanest-first.

```{python}
#| echo: false
elec_sources = [
    ("coal_share_elec", "Coal", FUEL_COLORS["coal"]),
    ("oil_share_elec", "Oil", FUEL_COLORS["oil"]),
    ("gas_share_elec", "Natural Gas", FUEL_COLORS["gas"]),
    ("nuclear_share_elec", "Nuclear", FUEL_COLORS["nuclear"]),
    ("renewables_share_elec", "Renewables (incl. hydro)", FUEL_COLORS["renewables"]),
]
if os.path.exists(emix_path):
    emix = pd.read_csv(emix_path)
    latest_year = emix.dropna(subset=["fossil_share_elec"]).groupby("country_code")["year"].max()
    rows = [emix[(emix["country_code"] == cc) & (emix["year"] == yr)].iloc[0]
            for cc, yr in latest_year.items()
            if not emix[(emix["country_code"] == cc) & (emix["year"] == yr)].empty]
    latest = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values("fossil_share_elec", ascending=False)

    fig = go.Figure()
    for col, name, color in elec_sources:
        fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
            y=latest["country_name"], x=latest[col].fillna(0),
            name=name, orientation="h", marker_color=color,
            hovertemplate=f"{name}: %{{x:.1f}}%<extra>%{{y}}</extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(
        barmode="stack", xaxis_title="% of electricity generation", yaxis_title="",
        plot_bgcolor="white", xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0", range=[0, 100]),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="top", y=-0.09, xanchor="center", x=0.5),
        height=680, margin=dict(b=180, t=20),
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: Our World in Data (Energy Institute, Ember) | Data as of: {get_data_date(emix_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.26,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Electricity mix data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Canada's electricity mix over time

How Canada generates electricity has shifted over the decades — coal phasing down, with hydro and nuclear supplying most of the grid.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(emix_path):
    ce = emix[emix["country_code"] == "CAN"].dropna(subset=["fossil_share_elec"]).sort_values("year")
    fig = go.Figure()
    for col, name, color in elec_sources:
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=ce["year"], y=ce[col].fillna(0), name=name, mode="lines",
            stackgroup="one", line=dict(width=0.5, color=color), fillcolor=color,
            hovertemplate=f"{name}: %{{y:.1f}}%<extra></extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(
        xaxis_title="", yaxis_title="% of electricity generation",
        yaxis=dict(range=[0, 100], gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0",
                   rangeslider=dict(visible=True, thickness=0.08, bgcolor="#f5f5f5"),
                   range=[1999, int(ce["year"].max())]),
        plot_bgcolor="white",
        legend=dict(orientation="v", yanchor="top", y=1, xanchor="left", x=1.02),
        margin=dict(b=90, t=20, r=190),
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: Our World in Data (Energy Institute, Ember) | Data as of: {get_data_date(emix_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.28,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Electricity mix data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Electricity mix by province

Canada has no single national grid — electricity is a provincial responsibility, and the mix varies enormously from one province to the next. Quebec, Manitoba, British Columbia, and Newfoundland & Labrador run almost entirely on hydro; Ontario leans heavily on nuclear. Among the provinces that still burn fossil fuels, the *type* matters: coal emits roughly twice the CO2 of natural gas per unit of electricity, so it is shown separately. Nova Scotia and Saskatchewan still lean on coal, while Alberta has largely switched from coal to gas. This is generation (actual output), not installed capacity.

```{python}
#| echo: false
prov_elec_path = "data/environment/statcan_provincial_electricity.csv"
prov_sources = [
    ("Hydro", FUEL_COLORS["hydro"]),
    ("Nuclear", FUEL_COLORS["nuclear"]),
    ("Wind", FUEL_COLORS["wind"]),
    ("Solar", FUEL_COLORS["solar"]),
    ("Biomass", FUEL_COLORS["biomass"]),
    ("Natural gas", FUEL_COLORS["gas"]),
    ("Oil", FUEL_COLORS["oil"]),
    ("Coal", FUEL_COLORS["coal"]),
]
if os.path.exists(prov_elec_path):
    pe = pd.read_csv(prov_elec_path)
    wide = pe.pivot(index="province", columns="source", values="share").fillna(0)
    for name, _ in prov_sources:
        if name not in wide.columns:
            wide[name] = 0
    # cleanest-first: most fossil at the bottom, least at the top (coal stacked last/rightmost)
    wide["_fossil"] = wide[["Natural gas", "Oil", "Coal"]].sum(axis=1)
    wide = wide.sort_values("_fossil", ascending=False)

    fig = go.Figure()
    for name, color in prov_sources:
        fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
            y=wide.index, x=wide[name], name=name, orientation="h",
            marker_color=color,
            hovertemplate=f"{name}: %{{x:.1f}}%<extra>%{{y}}</extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(
        barmode="stack", xaxis_title="% of electricity generation", yaxis_title="",
        plot_bgcolor="white", xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0", range=[0, 100]),
        yaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=11)),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="center", x=0.5),
        height=560, margin=dict(b=120, t=90, l=140),
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: Statistics Canada 25-10-0015-01 & 25-10-0084-01 | Data as of: {get_data_date(prov_elec_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.18, xanchor="left",
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Provincial electricity data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Total energy mix in Canada

Electricity is only part of the picture. This shows Canada's **total primary energy** — including the oil and gas burned for transport and heating, not just electricity — which is why fossil fuels still dominate even though the grid is mostly low-carbon.

```{python}
#| echo: false
energy_sources = [
    ("coal_share_energy", "Coal", FUEL_COLORS["coal"]),
    ("oil_share_energy", "Oil", FUEL_COLORS["oil"]),
    ("gas_share_energy", "Natural Gas", FUEL_COLORS["gas"]),
    ("nuclear_share_energy", "Nuclear", FUEL_COLORS["nuclear"]),
    ("renewables_share_energy", "Renewables (incl. hydro)", FUEL_COLORS["renewables"]),
]
if os.path.exists(emix_path):
    canada_emix = emix[emix["country_code"] == "CAN"].dropna(subset=["coal_share_energy"]).sort_values("year")
    fig = go.Figure()
    for col, name, color in energy_sources:
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=canada_emix["year"], y=canada_emix[col].fillna(0), name=name, mode="lines",
            stackgroup="one", line=dict(width=0.5, color=color), fillcolor=color,
            hovertemplate=f"{name}: %{{y:.1f}}%<extra></extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(
        xaxis_title="", yaxis_title="% of primary energy supply",
        yaxis=dict(range=[0, 100], gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0",
                   rangeslider=dict(visible=True, thickness=0.08, bgcolor="#f5f5f5"),
                   range=[1999, int(canada_emix["year"].max())]),
        plot_bgcolor="white",
        legend=dict(orientation="v", yanchor="top", y=1, xanchor="left", x=1.02),
        margin=dict(b=90, t=20, r=190),
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: Our World in Data (Energy Institute) | Data as of: {get_data_date(emix_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.28,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Energy mix data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Download data

- [GHG Emissions, national total (CSV)](../data/environment/eccc_ghg.csv) — ECCC National Inventory Report (CESI)
- [GHG Emissions by Economic Sector (CSV)](../data/environment/eccc_ghg_by_sector.csv) — ECCC (CESI)
- [CO2 per Capita (CSV)](../data/environment/oecd_co2_per_capita.csv) — OECD Green Growth Indicators
- [CO2 Emissions Indexed (CSV)](../data/environment/oecd_co2_indexed.csv) — OECD Green Growth Indicators
- [Consumption-Based CO2 per Capita (CSV)](../data/environment/owid_consumption_co2.csv) — Our World in Data (Global Carbon Project)
- [CO2 Emissions Intensity per GDP (CSV)](../data/environment/owid_co2_per_gdp.csv) — Our World in Data (Global Carbon Project)
- [CO2 per Capita — Global Context (CSV)](../data/environment/owid_co2_global_context.csv) — Our World in Data (Global Carbon Project)
- [Energy Mix — electricity & primary (CSV)](../data/environment/owid_energy_mix.csv) — Our World in Data (Energy Institute, Ember)
- [Electricity Generation per Capita (CSV)](../data/environment/owid_electricity_per_capita.csv) — Our World in Data (Energy Institute, Ember, EIA)
- [Provincial Electricity Generation (CSV)](../data/environment/statcan_provincial_electricity.csv) — Statistics Canada 25-10-0015-01
